item.title: ... and its also a core aspect of the game, you know?
item.description: The game rewards cooperation and tactical acumen, its rewarding to be able to pull off a tricky situation without killing your squadmates. EDIT: That was fast! Thanks for the upvotes, everyone! FOR SUPER EARTH! (I wanna review that game but so far I'm waiting for a review key, or a discount) I do some reviews as a hobby, if you wanna check them, feel free to visit https://hypeimmune.wordpress.com/
item.dc:creator: Skullwriter

item.title: The 419th vol. of "I'm not actually removing these from my phone, I just wanted to share them so I could feel some sort of connection in this dismal world" Dumps -- ND & Therapy Memes ep 9
item.description:
A lot of us are going to feel attacked by some of these MeeMays. Full disclosure, I'm diagnosed with Depression, ADHD, OCD and ASD.
STAY TUNED! I'll be releasing more MeeMays back into the wild *every other* Monday-Friday around 11:30 am, GMT-5, in my ""I'm not actually removing these from my phone, I just wanted to share them so that I could feel some sort of connection in this dismal world" Dumps" Or use Imgur's "Follow" Feature, which will alert you whenever one of my dumps goes Viral.
Me
This is my newly commissioned Signature Image, by Dark Arts Studios. He's no longer on The Imgurs, I met him on ElbowDeepInAHorse's Discord Server. Here's where you can see his work: https://twitter.com/Anubis23342060 https://www.furaffinity.net/user/thed4rk1ord/ https://tailsofthegods.com/ https://dark-arts.itch.io/ FUN TIP / LIFE FACT / PRO HACK I upvote 90% - 99% of positive or neutral comments on my posts, as well as ≈50% of negative ones Shoutout to my fellow Dumpers: @DeafKitten @CodyBurkett @OhValleyOfPlenty @AbbiiStabbii @DigiT00l @MemeMuseumCurator @CeleryMan7 @DumpsterKoala @JebidiahBeetus @Kaeldra @KirbyVictorious @HermioneDanger00 @DumpReview @WhiskeyJelly @JasonPositivityPilot @danat101 @PotatoCheesMonk @VanessaNelson @NotMuchBiggerThanAWomprat @MindNumbingBraIncAndy @TheMellowMoron @TheWoodsAreVile @ThatOtherGirlYouKnow @IStealMemesAndStuff @DrWalters @DaymanAhAAAAah Lemme know If you are a Regular Dumper and would like to be added to the list! Archive Of Previous Dumps: Vols 1-300: https://imgur.com/a/vAfF9Wz Vols 301+ : https://imgur.com/a/oDakPaq Updated Whenever I Can Get Around To It #MemeDump #NeuroDivergent #Therapy #MeeMays #AdolfHitlerSchoolForTheArts #TheWhoring20s #SaveTheBEEEEEES #BeeLM #SaveTheBees #JettfreyEpsfuelCandnt #CommeltSteelBeamicide #WhatIfNobodyShotJFK #AndHisHeadJustDidThat #EatingAppsTakingNaps #EatNamesAndTakeAss #MasturbatingIsNecrophilia #IfYoureDeadInside #DumpyMcGeeAndGuyNamedSteve #2Dumps1Site #AllTiddiesAreGoodTiddies #FreeTheNipple

item.title: So, I finished this.
item.description:
I've had this in my watch list for ages, and this month I finally got around to it, movie included. It seemed the internet unanimously agreed the show was a masterpiece, and long deserving of another season. No matter the website, its name would always pop up if ever the topic of cancelled shows reared its head. So, I went in, hoping for a nice space opera to get me through the last weeks of Canadian winter. Suffice to say, it delivered. Fun characters, engaging plots, intriguing worldbuilding, and gorgeous set design. A certified gem. I'll admit I had some minor issues and criticisms here and there, but overall I really enjoyed what I had watched. I also think I'm one of the few people who're completely content with it ending where it did. I don't need an undying franchise like Star Wars or the MCU; one season of high-quality episodes and a nice movie to top things off is just fine with me. It's not unlike Cowboy Bebop in that respect. Good show, would recommend. Now, if you'll excuse me...
Also Nandi is best girl. Don't @ me.
item.dc:creator: MrSwissroll

item.dc:creator: countervail247365

item.title: Eugene Levy's movie son Jason Biggs fills in for his real son at Walk of Fame ceremony
item.description:
Eugene Levy's son and Schitt's Creek collaborator, Dan Levy, wasn't able to attend his father's Hollywood Walk of Fame induction ceremony — but the elder Levy got the next best thing. At the unveiling Friday, his screen son Jason Biggs, who starred as James "Jim" Levenstein in four American Pie movies opposite Levy, was on hand to help honor him. "My son Daniel couldn't be here today, sadly," said Levy, 77. "He's shooting a movie in Bulgaria, and tell me that doesn't sound like an episode right out of Schitt's Creek. But my second son, Jim, is here from American Pie, and I love the fact you're here, Jason, honestly." Friday also marked a reunion for Levy and Catherine O'Hara, whom he worked with on Schitt's Creek and more projects over the years, including numerous Christopher Guest films. "We all know and love Eugene Levy for the wealth of original, thought-provoking, heartfelt, and ridiculously hilarious entertainment he has gifted us for so many years," O'Hara said. "Eugene is a gentleman in every sense of the word. Eugene takes his comedy very seriously… He's obviously funny, but he's not afraid to laugh at himself. In fact, most of his self-effacing jokes are aimed at the fact that he doesn't think he's funny at all — which is ridiculous."
Accepting his star on the Walk of Fame, Levy reflected on his humble beginnings. "If you know me at all, you know just how uncomfortable a day this is for me, knowing you've all uprooted your schedules to be here," he said. "Getting a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame is about as far from Hamilton, Ontario, Canada, as you can get. My hometown Hamilton was a big steel town when I was growing up, and if you did not want to spend your life in the steel mills or working in the scrap metal business or selling suits in one of Hamilton's fine haberdasheries, you had to get an education and become a doctor or a dentist or a lawyer or an engineer or an accountant. But nobody went into the entertainment business; It just didn't happen in Hamilton in the 1960s." But, Levy continued, "that's when I got hooked on this business, 1961, when I performed for the first time on stage at Central High. It was a production of The Taming of the Shrew, and I played the tailor. It was a small part, I only had one scene, but I remember getting a few laughs in that scene that I'm sure Shakespeare had not intended. But even then the sound of audience laughter had a big appeal for me. So, cutting to the chase here, that's how I spent the next 50-plus years working in comedy. How rewarding was that? A life spent making people laugh." https://ew.com/eugene-levy-movie-son-jason-biggs-walk-of-fame-ceremony-8606684 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KAVarFHTAZY And his new travel series: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oqM0ZYnA02g
item.dc:creator: Jbelkin

item.title: For Me to Laugh at Later 37
item.description:
I have no idea what this is referencing, but its so random I love it.
I like to think the dragon is wondering why this girl is wearing her skirt backwards.
Use your 1st amendment rights people.
I have a lot of questions.
I have seen/heard a lot of weird opinions last few years, but this is the weirdest.
Should be a gator holding a gun.

item.title: Freddie's grave
item.description: On Saturday I posted that my beloved tiny cat Freddie was gone. Thank you all for being very kind at the time. I just wanted to show her grave now I've finished it. I still miss her a lot but at least she has pride of place in the garden now.
